title: Returning to Tech With Confidence
summary: Rebuild confidence, set realistic goals, and understand the current hiring landscape for returners in IT.
description: Kick off the program by naming your strengths, reframing the career break, and setting a realistic plan for the next eight weeks.
speakerBio: Career returnship mentor and former IT program manager focused on helping women re-enter technology roles after a break.

agenda.title: Welcome and participant introductions
agenda.details: Set expectations, share goals, and create a supportive cohort dynamic for the eight-session journey.

agenda.title: Common return-to-work barriers in tech
agenda.details: Discuss confidence gaps, changing tools, and how to reframe the career break in a strengths-based way.

agenda.title: Goal-setting for the 8-session journey
agenda.details: Choose one target role and define simple weekly actions that can be sustained alongside existing commitments.

title: Resume Refresh for IT Roles
summary: Learn how to structure a resume for today's IT roles, including ATS-style formatting and stronger bullet writing.
description: Translate earlier experience into modern IT language and leave with a resume draft targeted to a realistic role family.
speakerBio: Technical recruiter and resume coach who partners with returners on positioning transferable skills for analyst, QA, and support roles.

agenda.title: Resume layout and ATS-style basics
agenda.details: Review formatting choices that improve readability for recruiters and applicant tracking systems.

agenda.title: Writing impact-focused experience bullets
agenda.details: Convert past responsibilities into stronger accomplishment statements with clear actions and outcomes.

agenda.title: Tailoring resumes for target job roles
agenda.details: Align summary, keywords, and selected achievements with the role family chosen in session one.

title: LinkedIn and Personal Branding
summary: Refresh your professional story online and present a confident, current profile to recruiters and hiring managers.
description: Update your digital presence with a stronger headline, a clear summary, and a light-touch visibility plan you can maintain weekly.
speakerBio: Brand strategist and LinkedIn trainer who helps mid-career professionals restart their job search with clarity and confidence.

agenda.title: LinkedIn headline and summary refresh
agenda.details: Draft a modern profile headline and About section that connect prior experience with current goals.

agenda.title: Showing career breaks with clarity and confidence
agenda.details: Use honest, concise language that explains the break without diminishing professional value.

agenda.title: Building visibility with small weekly actions
agenda.details: Create a low-pressure networking routine built around profile updates, comments, and reconnecting with warm contacts.

title: Business Analyst and Product Roles
summary: Explore common expectations for analyst and product-adjacent roles, including stakeholder work, documentation, and delivery support.
description: See how business analysis and product support roles map to transferable strengths such as communication, coordination, and structured problem solving.
speakerBio: Senior business analyst who has coached career returners into BA, product coordinator, and operations roles.

agenda.title: What BAs and product coordinators do day to day
agenda.details: Map meetings, backlog support, requirements gathering, and follow-up communication to common job descriptions.

agenda.title: Common tools, terms, and deliverables
agenda.details: Review user stories, acceptance criteria, process maps, and stakeholder notes in plain language.

agenda.title: How to position transferable experience
agenda.details: Identify examples from prior work that show facilitation, analysis, documentation, and delivery support.

title: QA and Testing Careers
summary: Understand manual QA, test case design, bug reporting, and how testing roles can be a strong re-entry path into IT.
description: Practice the language of quality assurance and learn how structured testing work can provide a credible, lower-barrier path back into tech.
speakerBio: QA lead and test manager with experience mentoring first-time testers and professionals returning to software delivery teams.

agenda.title: Core QA responsibilities and workflows
agenda.details: Walk through manual test execution, defect tracking, regression cycles, and collaboration with developers.

agenda.title: Writing test cases and reporting defects
agenda.details: Practice clear, reproducible bug reports and simple test cases that demonstrate structured thinking.

agenda.title: Translating attention to detail into resume language
agenda.details: Show how process discipline and quality checks from prior roles transfer into QA positioning.

title: Data, Reporting, and Power BI
summary: Learn the basics of reporting roles, dashboards, metrics, and how data storytelling appears in modern job descriptions.
description: Demystify reporting careers by walking through KPIs, dashboard language, and small skill-building steps that make data roles feel approachable.
speakerBio: Analytics manager and Power BI practitioner focused on helping nontraditional candidates break into reporting and operations analytics work.

agenda.title: Common data and reporting responsibilities
agenda.details: Outline recurring reporting tasks, stakeholder requests, metric definitions, and dashboard maintenance.

agenda.title: Intro to dashboards, KPIs, and Power BI language
agenda.details: Explain common reporting vocabulary so job postings feel more approachable and less technical.

agenda.title: First steps to bridge reporting skill gaps
agenda.details: Recommend realistic starter projects and learning habits that support a reporting-focused job search.

title: Cloud and DevOps Fundamentals
summary: Get comfortable with common cloud and deployment terms that appear in many current IT job postings.
description: Build confidence with cloud vocabulary, release pipelines, and realistic ways to show beginner exposure without overselling experience.
speakerBio: Cloud engineer and DevOps educator who designs beginner-friendly learning plans for early-career and returning professionals.

agenda.title: Cloud platforms and core concepts
agenda.details: Cover environments, storage, compute, and identity in beginner-friendly language tied to everyday IT work.

agenda.title: CI/CD, environments, and release basics
agenda.details: Demystify pipelines, deployments, testing environments, and why these terms appear across many roles.

agenda.title: How to show beginner exposure credibly
agenda.details: Discuss labs, coursework, and supporting examples that show curiosity without overstating expertise.

title: Interview Preparation and Next Steps
summary: Practice clearer interview answers, build confidence, and leave with a concrete plan for the next 30 days.
description: Close the series with mock interview practice, stronger storytelling, and a personal action plan for applications, networking, and follow-up learning.
speakerBio: Career returnship mentor and former IT program manager focused on helping women re-enter technology roles after a break.

agenda.title: Common return-to-work interview questions
agenda.details: Practice responses to questions about career breaks, readiness, and motivations for returning now.

agenda.title: Structuring answers with confidence
agenda.details: Use simple storytelling frameworks to give concise, evidence-based answers in interviews.

agenda.title: Personal next-step plan after the program
agenda.details: Leave with a 30-day action plan covering applications, networking, and continued skill-building.
